title: angelica heterocarpa
intro: Angelica heterocarpa, also known as Japanese angelica or Korean angelica, is a species of plant in the carrot family, native to East Asia and used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Angelica heterocarpa are attributed to its high content of coumarins, volatile oils, and other bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of consuming Angelica heterocarpa include reducing inflammation and pain, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Angelica heterocarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in some Asian dishes.
